states.description: My Tasks main list view showing tasks with due date chips and priority-colored checkboxes
Detailed editor view of a single task (not clearly entered in these episodes)

strategies.name: QUERY_TASKS_BY_DATE
steps: On task_list, scroll through the entire list to enumerate date chips (Today/Tmrw/Yest/Mon..Sun/Oct N)
Map natural-language date ('today is Oct 15') to chip labels
Collect task titles whose chip matches the target date
Return titles as a comma-separated list, or 'None' if empty
strategies.success_signal: Answer action returns the exact set of task titles for the date
strategies.fallback: If date cannot be resolved to a visible chip, open the task to inspect its full date

strategies.name: QUERY_INCOMPLETE_BY_DEADLINE
steps: Identify tasks whose checkbox is UNCHECKED and whose date chip is on or before the target day
strategies.success_signal: Answer contains all and only incomplete tasks with dates <= target

strategies.name: QUERY_HIGH_PRIORITY
steps: Identify tasks whose checkbox is colored RED (high priority indicator in this app)
strategies.fallback: Do not infer priority from due date proximity or other colors

dead_ends.failed_action: repeatedly answer and re-answer without calling status(complete)
dead_ends.note: Agent loops emitting 'answer' actions; must emit status(complete) once answer is given

dead_ends.failed_action: interpret 'Yest' chip as the queried future/specific date
dead_ends.note: Relative date chips (Yest/Today/Tmrw/Mon..Sun) must be mapped to absolute dates using the current date; mis-mapping produces wrong answers

dead_ends.failed_action: guess high priority from due-date urgency or blue checkbox
dead_ends.note: Only RED checkbox indicates high priority; blue/yellow are not high priority

dead_ends.failed_action: scroll repeatedly looking for an absolute date that is not present
dead_ends.note: List does not expose arbitrary future dates if no task exists; infinite scroll loops waste steps
Sort menu has no priority filter; opening it does not help priority/date queries
Agent declared infeasible without scrolling entire list to confirm no matching tasks

categories.name: QUERY_INFO
categories.precondition: Target data-bearing screen reachable from launcher; query specifies a filter (date, status, priority, etc.)
abstract_steps: Launch the target app from the launcher
Navigate to the primary list/overview screen
Resolve any relative terms in the query (today, next week, after next) to absolute values using the device/task context
Scroll through the full list to enumerate all candidate items
Apply the query's filter predicate to each item using observable visual attributes
Aggregate matching items into the requested output format (list, count, yes/no)
Emit a single answer action, then immediately emit a terminal completion signal
failure_modes: Confusing relative date labels with the queried absolute date
Answering from a partial view without scrolling the full list
Repeating the same answer action without terminating, causing step exhaustion
Guessing semantic attributes (priority, category) from unrelated visual cues
Declaring the query infeasible before exhausting available navigation (scroll, open detail)
Miscounting due to duplicated or partially visible rows after scrolling
verification_checklist: Did I reach the list screen that actually contains the data?
Did I map all relative terms to concrete values consistent with the current date?
Did I scroll until the end of the list (no new content appears)?
Does each returned item satisfy every part of the filter (date AND status AND priority)?
Is the answer format exactly what the user requested (comma-separated / count / None)?
Did I emit a terminal completion action exactly once after answering?

categories.name: FILTER_BY_ATTRIBUTE
categories.precondition: List view exposes per-item visual attributes (color, icon, label) that encode the attribute of interest
abstract_steps: Identify the concrete visual encoding of the target attribute on this app (e.g., checkbox color = priority)
Iterate items and apply the encoding rule
failure_modes: Assuming a default encoding from other apps rather than observing this app's cues
Treating urgency/date as priority when priority is encoded separately
verification_checklist: Is the attribute encoding confirmed by at least one unambiguous example?
Are negative examples (items that should NOT match) excluded?

categories.name: NAVIGATE_TO
abstract_steps: Invoke the app-launch primitive with the app name
Verify the landing screen matches expectations before acting
failure_modes: Acting before the landing screen is fully loaded
verification_checklist: Title bar/landmark of destination screen is visible
